data.body:
PLAN-REVIEW VALIDATION of the prerequisite-realignment edits (manual harness run, 2026-06).

Ran the prototype three-pass gate (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/final_gate_3pass.py, v8 rubric, all 31 overlays forced on, agentic Pass-2) on the UPDATED epic 5fd2 (42.2k chars) + its 13 live children, to validate the realignment edits.

OUTCOME: DET floor PASS (check-ac 19 lines; clarity 7/5). Three-pass: 29 Pass-1 findings -> Pass-3: 0 BLOCK (v1 advisory posture), 10 advisory, 19 dropped. No blocking findings; the edits did not regress the gate.

The advisory set is dominated by PRE-EXISTING epic-level design questions unrelated to this realignment (T8 unspecified priority-score formula; T12 overlay-tier consistency; G4 ISF scope overlap between children 681b and 7284; Pass-2 single-aggregate-vs-per-finding wording; T5a container fan-out cost). These are the same advisory themes the gate has always surfaced on this epic and are out of scope for the prerequisite update.

The ONLY finding attributable to the realignment edits was E1 (dropped, conf 0.43): terminology drift on P2/P3 tooling — I'd updated the design-summary polyglot bullet + carve-out + child 012e to the oracle/ctags reality but left the authoritative criteria-registry section and the top-level AC list still naming the old 'LSP/ast-grep' / 'syft+registry+fallback'. FIXED: both now name the 8f6c oracle (universal-ctags T1; deps.dev+syft T0; LSP/solidlsp = T2/850f). Re-verified: zero remaining stale DET tool refs; check-ac + clarity still pass.

Raw harness output: tmp/final_gate_3pass.json.
